statsbomb_open_data.pit_blocker: Open-data match_available and update timestamps describe public dataset availability, not proof that the event feed was available at every historical pre-match freeze.

wyscout_figshare.coverage: One entire season for La Liga, Serie A, Bundesliga, Premier League and Ligue 1, plus FIFA World Cup 2018 and UEFA Euro 2016.
wyscout_figshare.reason: One club season per league is insufficient for the frozen minimum three-season chronological validation and does not provide historical pre-match source-observation timestamps.

metrica_sample_data.coverage: Two anonymized matches with sample tracking and event data.
metrica_sample_data.reason: Sample size and anonymization prevent competition-specific chronological training.

skillcorner_open_data.coverage: Ten 2024/25 Australian A-League broadcast-tracking matches, with dynamic events and phase-of-play samples.
skillcorner_open_data.reason: Ten sample matches outside the 17 registered domains cannot support competition-specific validation.

integrated_elite_soccer_sample.coverage: Seven official German Bundesliga first/second-division matches.
integrated_elite_soccer_sample.reason: Research sample only; far below minimum match and season gates.

reopen_requires: licensed or official multi-season event feed for a registered competition
immutable raw snapshots with original observed-at timestamps
at least three chronological seasons and one thousand matches after quality filtering
policy: Open data may be used only for clearly labelled retrospective algorithm prototyping. No routine single-match probability, score, total-goal, handicap, direction or EV output may use an event-value layer until a competition-specific PIT-safe promotion is completed.
